PERSHING GENERAL HOSPITAL SNF

CCN: 295000 · LOVELOCK, NV 89419 · Pershing County

Overview

Address
855 6TH STREET, LOVELOCK, NV 89419
Phone
7752732621
Certified beds
25
Avg daily residents
25 (100% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (hospital district)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1967-01-01
Setting
Rural
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.95 3.05 5.00 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.03 0.53 1.30 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.48 0.68 0.61 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.44 1.84 3.09 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.51 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.07
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.50
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.89
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
59.0%
annual
RN turnover
58.3%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.08
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.78
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-17 3 3 0 12 1 12
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-18 23 9 14 96 1 96
Total weighted health score
33.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (42)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
41 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-09
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-09
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-09
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-10
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-16
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. B 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-04-15 2024-04-20
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. E 2024-04-15 2024-04-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-15
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-13
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. E 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-17
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. E 2024-04-15 2024-05-13
0945 Include as part of its infection prevention and control program, mandatory training that includes written standards, policies, and procedures for the program. D 2024-04-15 2024-04-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-04-15 2024-05-15
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2024-04-15 2024-04-23
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-02-28 2024-03-18
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-02-28 2024-03-21
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-02-28 2024-03-21
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-02-28 2024-03-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-02-28 2024-03-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-10-19 2023-10-23
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-10-19 2023-10-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-10-19 2023-10-19
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2023-04-20 2023-06-05

Methodology & sources
